codeReview.summary: The original dialogue tree feature previously failed because none of the authored branches actually granted rewards. In the current repo state, the later fix commit adds reward-bearing choices to all five dialogue trees while preserving the original branching, prerequisite gating, scrollable UI, and save/load wiring, so the feature now satisfies the required alternate outcomes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Cross-scene persistence via UIScene proxy methods is now used for dialogue state, but the shared architecture notes still only document the companion proxy methods.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Dialogue state is saved through ZoneScene.getDialogueState()/setDialogueState() calling UIScene methods in src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts:2805-2818 and src/scenes/UIScene.ts:1879-1885, while .factory/library/architecture.md only documents companion cross-scene methods.

summary: Verdict: pass. I reviewed the original dialogue-trees commit, the prior failed review context, the worker transcript skeleton, and the current code. The follow-up fix commit 4ebb038 adds reachable reward outcomes in all five authored dialogue trees, making the originally missing reward/branching requirement satisfy the feature spec.
